symptoms: spindle-shaped leaf spots
gray or white center on spots
brown margin on leaves
rotted or black neck node
empty or unfilled grain heads
favorable_conditions: High relative humidity (>90%), cool night temperatures (20-25°C), and excessive nitrogen (Urea) application.
organic_treatment: Apply wood ash to field, spray neem seed kernel extract (5%), ensure proper field drainage, and maintain recommended water level.
chemical_treatment: Apply Tricyclazole 75% WP (0.6g/L) or Isoprothiolane 40% EC (1.5ml/L) as recommended by Department of Agriculture Sri Lanka.
prevention: Avoid over-application of Urea fertilizer; use blast-resistant varieties like Bg 300, Bg 352, or Bg 379/2.

disease_name: Brown Planthopper Damage (Kalu Kapanawa / Nilaparvata lugens)
symptoms: yellowing of lower leaves
browning and drying of plants in circular patches
hopperburn (burnt appearance of crop)
sooty mold on stem bases
favorable_conditions: Continuous high water level, dense crop canopy, cloudy weather, and overuse of broad-spectrum insecticides.
organic_treatment: Drain field water completely for 3-4 days to expose pest base; encourage natural predators (spiders, mirid bugs).
chemical_treatment: Apply Pymetrozine 50% WG or Buprofezin 25% SC at early nymph stages under Agrarian Instructor guidance.
prevention: Maintain proper plant spacing (20cm x 15cm), practice alternate wetting and drying (AWD).

disease_name: Chilli Leaf Curl Virus (Kuda Veyiya / Vector: Thrips & Whitefly)
symptoms: upward curling of leaves
puckered and thickened leaf blades
stunted plant growth
reduced leaf size and clustering
poor fruit set or small distorted chillies
favorable_conditions: Hot, dry weather spells favoring rapid vector insect multiplication.
organic_treatment: Spray neem oil emulsion (5ml/L + soap solution) or garlic-chilli extract to reduce vector thrips/whiteflies.
chemical_treatment: Target vectors with Imidacloprid 200 SL (0.5ml/L) or Acetamiprid 20% SP under local agricultural advisory.
prevention: Plant 3-4 rows of maize or sorghum as a living barrier around chilli plots; install yellow and blue sticky traps.

disease_name: Anthracnose / Fruit Rot (Gedipala Veyiya / Colletotrichum capsici)
symptoms: circular sunken spots on ripe fruits
black concentric rings on chilli pods
dried or straw-colored ripe fruits
dieback of upper branches
favorable_conditions: High humidity (>80%) accompanied by rain showers during fruit ripening.
organic_treatment: Remove and burn infected fruits immediately; spray copper hydroxide or Trichoderma viride formulations.
chemical_treatment: Spray Mancozeb 80% WP (2g/L) or Difenoconazole 250 EC (0.5ml/L) at fruit formation stage.
prevention: Use pathogen-free seeds; treat seeds with Thiram before sowing.

symptoms: water-soaked dark green/brown spots on leaves
white cottony fungal growth on underside of leaves
brown firm spots on green and ripe fruits
rapid wilting of plant canopy
favorable_conditions: Cool temperatures (15-22°C) combined with high rainfall or heavy dew (common in Nuwara Eliya & Badulla).
organic_treatment: Prune lower leaves touching soil; apply copper sulfate + lime mixture (Bordeaux mixture).
chemical_treatment: Spray Metalaxyl 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP (2.5g/L) or Dimethomorph 50% WP.
prevention: Ensure wide spacing; avoid overhead sprinkler irrigation.

disease_name: Cinnamon Rough Bark / Leaf Blight (Colletotrichum gloeosporioides)
symptoms: brown irregular spots on young leaves
rough cracked bark on mature cinnamon stems
dieback of tender shoot tips
defoliation of young cinnamon bushes
favorable_conditions: Excessive shade, unpruned dense bushes, and high monsoon rainfall in Southern Sri Lanka.
organic_treatment: Prune overcrowded foliage to improve sunlight penetration; destroy infected harvest waste.
chemical_treatment: Apply Copper Oxychloride 50% WP (2.5g/L) following Department of Export Agriculture guidelines.
prevention: Maintain shade tree density at 20-25%; apply balanced NPK fertilizer.

symptoms: translucent pale yellow spots on tender leaves
white blister-like swellings on under-surface of flush
curling and distortion of young tea shoots
blackened dead flush
favorable_conditions: Monsoonal mist, cloudy weather with less than 3 hours of daily sunshine (Upcountry tea plantations).
organic_treatment: Adjust pruning schedule to avoid peak monsoon flush; increase shade tree lopping.
chemical_treatment: Spray Copper Oxychloride 50% WP combined with Nickel Chloride under TRI (Tea Research Institute) recommendations.
prevention: Monitor daily sunshine hours; use blister-resistant tea clones.

symptoms: ragged holes in young maize leaves
sawdust-like frass (excrement) inside leaf whorl
damaged growing points
damaged cobs with larval feeding
caterpillar infestation
favorable_conditions: Warm dry spells during early vegetative and tasseling stages in Anuradhapura, Monaragala, and Ampara.
organic_treatment: Apply neem seed kernel extract into whorls; drop sand/ash mixed with neem powder into central whorls.
chemical_treatment: Apply Spinetoram 11.7% SC or Emamectin Benzoate 5% SG as authorized by Sri Lanka DOA.
prevention: Synchronized planting in villages; crop rotation with non-host legumes.

disease_name: Potato Late Blight (Phytophthora infestans / Urani Paluwa)
symptoms: water-soaked dark spots on leaf tips and margins
white mildew/fungal web under leaf surfaces in humid weather
brown dry rot on potato tubers
rapid foliage collapse and foul odor
favorable_conditions: High relative humidity (>90%) with cool temperatures (15-20°C) during fog or rains in Nuwara Eliya & Welimada.
organic_treatment: Hilling up soil high around plant bases; spray Bordeaux mixture (1%) at weekly intervals.
chemical_treatment: Apply Mancozeb 80% WP (2.5g/L) or Cymoxanil + Mancozeb (2g/L) strictly according to DOA guidelines.
prevention: Plant disease-free certified seed potatoes; destroy volunteer host plants.

disease_name: Black Pepper Quick Wilt / Foot Rot (Phytophthora capsici)
symptoms: dark brown or black water-soaked lesions on leaf tips
sudden yellowing and flaccidity of entire vine canopy
blackening and sloughing off of bark on stem base (foot rot)
rapid shedding of leaves and pepper spikes within 7-14 days
favorable_conditions: Heavy monsoonal rains leading to waterlogging near stem bases in Matale, Kandy, and Kegalle.
organic_treatment: Ensure deep drainage contour channels; drench base with Trichoderma viride bio-fungicide mixture.
chemical_treatment: Drench vine root zone with Potassium Phosphonate (3ml/L) or Copper Oxychloride 50% WP (3g/L) at monsoon onset.
prevention: Tie vines above ground before rains; avoid root damage during weeding.
